title: oxalis corniculata var. repens
intro: Oxalis corniculata var. repens, commonly known as creeping oxalis or wood sorrel, is a perennial herb that belongs to the oxalis genus of flowering plants. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which help to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting against infections, and supporting the digestive system, among others. Oxalis corniculata var. repels can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption, and it is also sometimes used in traditional cooking, particularly in salads and as a natural source of vitamin C.
